chapter.title: Temporal H3 Cell Indices
chapter.para: The temporal H3 cell index type th3index represents the movement of objects as a sequence of H3 hexagonal cell identifiers. Uber's H3 is a discrete global grid system that tessellates the sphere into 122 base cells at resolution 0 and subdivides each cell into 7 children at every finer resolution (up to 15). Each cell has a 64-bit integer identifier that encodes the base cell, resolution, and hierarchical position.

The th3index type shares its on-disk representation with tbigint: both store a temporal 64-bit integer. The distinct SQL type exists purely to let the type-checker reject h3-specific functions when applied to arbitrary bigint trajectories (and vice-versa). Casts to / from tbigint are explicit (AS ASSIGNMENT) and binary-coercion only — zero-cost at runtime but requiring an explicit :: in SQL.

As with other temporal types, th3index has four subtypes: Instant, discrete-sequence, continuous-sequence with step interpolation (H3 cells are discrete — linear interpolation is meaningless), and SequenceSet.

sect1.title: Input and Output
sect1.para: A th3index value is entered using the standard temporal syntax with the underlying 64-bit H3 cell identifier. The input parser accepts both the canonical hexadecimal form (as produced by the H3 CLI and by h3_cell_to_string upstream) and the equivalent unsigned decimal. Hexadecimal is idiomatic in the H3 ecosystem and is what the output function emits — all examples below use it. For reference, the hex value 831c02fffffffff used in the examples corresponds to the decimal value 590464338553208831.
The type accepts type modifiers (typmod) to constrain a column to a specific subtype: Instant, Sequence, or SequenceSet.

The function arrowRoundtrip converts a temporal H3 index to the Arrow C Data Interface and reconstructs it, returning a value equal to the input. The Arrow C Data Interface is an in-process columnar ABI; this function validates the lossless correspondence and is the interop self-check reused by the language bindings. An H3 cell index is a 64-bit value, so it uses the unsigned 64-bit value leaf, distinct from the signed 64-bit leaf of temporal big integer.

sect1.title: Conversions
sect1.para: A th3index value shares the same on-disk representation as tbigint. The binary-coercion cast between them is explicit — requiring :: — so h3-specific functions cannot be silently called on arbitrary bigint trajectories.

listitem.para: Convert between a th3index and a tbigint (explicit, zero-cost)

Cast a temporal H3 cell to a temporal geodetic / planar point of cell centroids. The two overloads give the caller a choice between the geodetic (tgeogpoint) and SRID-4326 planar (tgeompoint) representations.
Return the first or last H3 cell identifier of a temporal value
Return the nth distinct H3 cell identifier (1-indexed). Returns NULL if out of range.
Return the set of distinct H3 cell identifiers the trajectory takes
Return the H3 cell identifier at a given timestamp, using step interpolation
Return the temporal resolution level (0–15) of each cell in a trajectory
Return the temporal base-cell number (0–121) of each cell in a trajectory
Return a temporal boolean stating at each instant whether the value is a valid H3 cell
Return a temporal boolean stating whether each cell has Class-III orientation. Class III alternates with resolution: odd resolutions are class III, even are class II.
Return a temporal boolean stating whether each cell is a pentagon (12 cells per resolution are pentagons, the remaining 110 at each base cell descend as hexagons)
Return the temporal parent cell at the given resolution. The no-argument form drops to the next-coarser resolution.
Return the temporal center-child cell at the given resolution. The no-argument form drops to the next-finer resolution.
Return the ordinal position (0-based) of each cell among the children of its parent at the given resolution
Return the temporal child cell at a given ordinal position of the given parent, at the given child resolution. The two temporal operands are synchronised over their shared time axis.
Return the temporal H3 cell at the given resolution that contains each point in a temporal geodetic or planar point (SRID must be 4326 for the tgeompoint overload)
Return the temporal geodetic centroid of each cell in a trajectory. A planar (SRID 4326) overload is available under the name h3_cell_to_latlng_tgeompoint.
Return the temporal polygon boundary of each cell in a trajectory, as a temporal geography

sect1.title: Directed Edges
sect1.para: H3 directed edges are themselves 64-bit identifiers (encoded differently from cell identifiers — h3_is_valid_directed_edge distinguishes them). The MobilityDB th3index type carries directed edges by convention: the same type, interpreted through edge-specific accessors.

listitem.para: Return a temporal boolean stating whether two temporal cells are grid neighbours at each instant. The two operands are synchronised.
Return a temporal directed-edge identifier from an origin/destination pair of temporal cells
Return a temporal boolean stating at each instant whether the value is a valid H3 directed edge
Return the temporal origin or destination cell of a temporal directed edge
Return the temporal polygon boundary of each directed edge in a trajectory, as a temporal geography
Return the temporal H3 vertex at the given vertex number (0–5 for hexagons, 0–4 for pentagons)
Return the temporal geodetic coordinates of each vertex in a trajectory
Return a temporal boolean stating at each instant whether the value is a valid H3 vertex

Return the temporal grid-hop distance (number of single-cell steps) between two temporal cells. Equivalently, the <-> operator returns the same distance. The two operands are synchronised over their shared time axis.

Convert between a temporal cell and a temporal local (I, J) coordinate pair relative to an origin temporal cell. The local coordinate is exposed as a tgeompoint with the I axis in the x slot and J in y.

sect1.title: Metrics
sect1.para: The three metric functions take an optional unit argument. For areas: km2 (default), m2, or rads2. For lengths: km (default), m, or rads. Passing an area unit to a length function (or vice-versa) raises an error.

listitem.para: Return the temporal area of each cell in a trajectory, in the requested unit
Return the temporal length of each directed edge in a trajectory, in the requested unit
Return the temporal great-circle distance between two temporal geodetic points (not between cells). The two operands are synchronised.

sect2.title: Ever and Always Comparisons
sect2.para: The probe operand may be a bare h3index H3 cell identifier or another th3index trajectory; both are supported in either operand position.

listitem.para: Return true if a temporal H3 cell is ever equal to the probe at at least one instant
Return true if a temporal H3 cell is always equal to the probe across its entire time axis
Return true if a temporal H3 cell is ever different from the probe
Return true if a temporal H3 cell is always different from the probe
Return a temporal boolean giving the per-instant equality (or inequality) between a temporal H3 cell and a probe

sect1.title: Bounding-Box Operators
sect1.para: The following operators compare the time extent of temporal H3 cells, not cell-hierarchy containment. th3index is classified as a time-only temporal type (like ttext / tbool): H3 cell ids have no meaningful total order, so the bounding box reduces to the trajectory's tstzspan period. The operators below compare that period. See the indexing section for the opclasses that make these operators index-capable.

note.para: The @> operator tests time-period containment, not H3 hierarchical containment. To test whether one cell is the parent of another at a given resolution, use h3_cell_to_parent.

listitem.para: Time-period overlap, contains, contained-by, same, and adjacent
Relative position along the time axis (strictly-before, overlaps-before, strictly-after, overlaps-after)

sect1.title: Set-Returning Helpers
sect1.para: The following static helpers return an h3indexset (or intset for h3_get_icosahedron_faces) — the finite-collection companion type of h3index. They operate on a static h3index (or an h3indexset) and are not lifted to th3index; the temporal lift is deferred pending a tset<T> primitive.

listitem.para: All cells within k grid steps of the origin (inclusive of the origin at k=0).
The cells at exactly k grid steps from the origin. Fails near pentagons.
The inclusive path of cells between two cells of the same resolution.
All children of a cell at the given target resolution.
Compacted representation of a cell set — finer cells are merged into their parent whenever a full hexagonal set of siblings is present.
Uncompacted representation at the given resolution — the inverse of h3_compact_cells.
All outgoing directed edges of a cell (6 for hexagons, 5 for pentagons).
All vertexes of a cell (6 for hexagons, 5 for pentagons).
The icosahedron face indexes (0..19) intersected by a cell.

sect1.title: Indexing
sect1.para: GiST and SP-GiST operator classes are defined so the bounding-box operators of the previous section can be used with corresponding indexes.

listitem.para: GiST operator class for temporal H3 cells. Indexes a tstzspan bounding period per row (th3index is a time-only temporal type).
listitem.programlisting: CREATE INDEX ON trips USING gist(cells); -- uses th3index_rtree_ops by default CREATE INDEX ON trips USING gist(cells th3index_rtree_ops);

listitem.para: SP-GiST operator classes for temporal H3 cells, keyed on the tstzspan period. th3index_quadtree_ops is the default (quadtree partitioning); th3index_kdtree_ops uses k-d tree partitioning and must be named explicitly.
listitem.programlisting: CREATE INDEX ON trips USING spgist(cells); -- uses th3index_quadtree_ops by default CREATE INDEX ON trips USING spgist(cells th3index_kdtree_ops);
